title: cli: contact resolve() treats any 21+ char string as a pubkey
description: cli/src/config.rs:122-125: `resolve()` treats any input longer than 20 chars as a raw pubkey. A contact named e.g. "team-engineering-lead" (22 chars) is silently passed as a pubkey, routing the message to a nonexistent destination with no error. Pubkeys are always exactly 64 lowercase hex chars; the fix is: `name_or_key.len() == 64 && name_or_key.chars().all(|c| c.is_ascii_hexdigit() && c.is_ascii_lowercase())` instead of the length-only check.
